metadata.description: Essential Chinese vocabulary for meeting people, social interactions, and polite conversation

content.name: interjection
content.side_a: (interjectory particle used to soften a question)
content.side_g: 呀 is a softening sentence-final particle, a phonetic variant of 啊 used after vowels ending in -a, -e, -i, -o, -ü. It lightens tone in greetings, questions, and exclamations; never use in formal writing.

content.name: enter
content.side_g: 进 is a directional verb often paired with a complement (进来 'come in', 进去 'go in'). Standalone 进 is common in signs and imperatives like 请进. Opposite is 出 (chū, exit).

content.name: fast_quick
content.side_g: 快 works as both adjective ('fast') and adverb ('quickly, hurry up'). As an urging adverb before a verb (快来, 快走) it conveys impatience or warmth. Contrast with 慢 (màn, slow); reduplication 快快 intensifies in children's speech.

content.name: come_in
content.side_f: Please come in and sit. (lit. 'Please enter-come sit')
content.side_g: 进来 is a verb+directional-complement compound: 进 (action) + 来 (toward speaker). Use 进去 if movement is away from speaker. The 来 is neutral tone in this compound. Objects go between: 进屋来 'come into the room'.

content.name: come
content.side_f: He is coming to my house tomorrow. (lit. 'He tomorrow come my home')
content.side_g: 来 indicates motion toward the speaker's location, opposite of 去 (qù). It also serves as a directional complement (进来, 回来) and a light verb meaning 'do/have' (来一杯 'have a cup'). Time words precede 来.

content.name: introduce
content.side_f: Let me introduce (briefly). (lit. 'I come introduce one-time')
content.side_g: 介绍 is a two-syllable verb meaning 'introduce' (people, products, topics). The pattern 我来介绍一下 is a fixed polite formula at first meetings. Object order: 给 A 介绍 B ('introduce B to A'). Can also be a noun: 自我介绍 'self-introduction'.

content.name: once_bit
content.side_g: 一下 after a verb softens the action and implies 'briefly, a bit', similar to reduplication (看看 ≈ 看一下). Tone sandhi: 一 shifts to yí before a 4th-tone syllable (xià). Very common for polite requests; more casual than 请.

content.name: happy_pleased
content.side_f: I'm happy to meet you. (lit. 'Know you, I very happy')
content.side_g: 高兴 describes momentary pleasure about a specific event; for lasting happiness use 快乐 (kuàilè) or 幸福 (xìngfú). Common pattern: 很高兴+VP ('glad to…'). As a verb it can take an object: 高兴什么 'what's there to be happy about?'

content.name: pretty
content.side_f: Your home is very pretty. (lit. 'Your home very pretty')
content.side_g: 漂亮 applies to people, places, objects, and even actions (一个漂亮的进球 'a beautiful goal'). The 亮 is neutral tone in this word. Synonyms: 美 (měi, more literary), 好看 (hǎokàn, colloquial 'good-looking'). Typically needs 很 or another degree adverb when predicative.
